title: VSEPR theory
short: Valence-Shell Electron-Pair Repulsion: electron groups around a central atom spread out as far apart as possible, setting the molecule's shape.
full:
VSEPR theory predicts molecular geometry by treating each bonding region and each lone pair around a central atom as an 'electron group' that repels the others. The groups arrange themselves to be as far apart as possible.

Two groups give a linear shape (180°), three give trigonal planar (120°), four give tetrahedral (109.5°). Lone pairs occupy more room than bonds, so they squeeze bond angles slightly and change the named shape — four groups with two lone pairs (as in water) give a bent molecule. Shape, together with bond polarity, decides whether the molecule is polar overall.
